rulings.Case Title: DANIEL PHILBIN ET AL VS. PETER S. BOYER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2024, Line 16. RESPONDENT PETER BOYER, TRUSTEE, AND MARGARET BOYER's PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION. Transferred to be heard in Department 501 on October 18, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all cases arising from landlord-tenant disputes per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Both parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date and department no later than October 7, 2024. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL QUINTANA VS. DUNG VU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2024, Line 1. DEFENDANT DUNG VU's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. The "motion for order to set aside default (CCP 473)" is denied without prejudice. This action regards a 2014 traffic accident. Defendant driver Dung Vu was served with summons in November 2019. Default and default judgment were entered against Vu on December 15, 2023. Vu's insurer says it "has been unable to contact him," but "can intervene" and move to lift the default judgment against Vu. (Memo. 2:9-10, 18-19.) However, the insurer has not moved to intervene. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: QUADRA & COLL, LLP ET AL VS. ABUL SHAH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2024, Line 17. DEFENDANT ABUL SHAH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant Abul Shah's motion for summary adjudication is denied. Shah's separate statement fails to comply with California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1350(d), (h). It does not separately identify each cause of action and each supporting material fact claimed to be in dispute. This impedes the court's ability to determine if each claim is disposed of in its entirety. Additionally, Shah concedes his request for summary adjudication as to the fifth cause of action is procedurally defective, as it does not dispose of the entire claim. (Reply 9:24-10:1.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DELMA LAMAR JEFFERSON VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Lift Stay On Civil Proceeings Plaintiffs Inavility To Continue Arbitration Due To Finicail Hardship
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2024, Line 6. 2 - PLAINTIFF DELMA JEFFERSON's MOTION To Lift Stay On Civil Proceeings Due To Plaintiff's Inability To Continue Arbitration Due To Financial Hardship. Plaintiff's "amended motion to lift stay on civil proceedings plaintiffs [sic] inability to continue arbitration due to finicail [sic] hardship" is denied without prejudice. This motion repeats the same arguments made in plaintiff's companion motion; they are rejected for the same reasons. Plaintiff also claims financial hardship, but defendant James River Insurance reports it has advanced all of plaintiff's arbitration fees. (Opp. 6:14-20.) rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2024, Line 11. 1 - DEFENDANT SHLOMO RECHNITZ, TOM WOOD, GEORGIA OTTERSON, ZORAIDA MARTINEZ, GINA FIZULIC, MARY KRISTINE LOUISE L. rulings.Case Title: DEBRA CURTIS VS. LAGUNA HONDA HOSPITAL AND REHABILITATION CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2024, Line 13. DEFENDANT LAGUNA HONDA HOSPITAL AND REHABILITATION CENTER's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. The City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to plaintiff's check-box complaint is sustained with leave to amend. The complaint does not plead "willful and unprovoked acts of aggression," a physical condition of property or ratification. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ZAHIR NASERI VS. OFFICE OF THE MAYOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Erroneously Entered Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2024, Line 14. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Set Aside Erroneously Entered Default. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL POWELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2024, Line 15. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND LONDON BREED's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to plaintiff's largely incomprehensible complaint is sustained with leave to file an understandable complaint that pleads causes of action. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely.
